steps.title: Let the starting number be N
steps.content: Let the initial number of prey and predators each be $N$.

steps.title: Compute the final counts
steps.content:
Prey increased by 2300% of $N$, so the increase is $23N$ and the final prey count is $N+23N = 24N$.
Predators increased by 200% of $N$, so the increase is $2N$ and the final predator count is $N+2N = 3N$.

steps.title: Find the percent by which prey exceeds predators
steps.content:
The ratio of final prey to final predators is $\dfrac{24N}{3N}=8$.
If prey is $p\%$ greater than predators, then $\dfrac{\text{prey}}{\text{predators}} = 1 + \frac{p}{100}$.
Thus $1 + \frac{p}{100}=8$, giving $\frac{p}{100}=7$ and $p=700$.
The answer is 700.
